ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic chicken sandwich entry (FOODON_00002473); no specific Vietnamese-style shredded chicken bánh mì entry exists. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a specific bánh mì sandwich composite code. A0FHR (cooked chicken meat) used as the closest defensible anchor for the dominant protein component.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code exists for a Vietnamese-style shredded chicken sandwich composite dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bánh mì sandwich weighing approximately 220 g, comprising a Vietnamese-style baguette filled with shredded chicken, mayonnaise, pickled vegetables, fresh herbs, and chilli paste.

nutrient_source.title: Chicken sandwich, with spread — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 21299) adapted with bánh mì component profile
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact HPB Singapore or USDA entry exists for a Vietnamese-style shredded chicken bánh mì sandwich. Values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy chicken sandwich data (NDB 21299, approximately 220 kcal/100 g) cross-referenced with USDA cooked shredded chicken breast (FDC ID 171477) and standard baguette bread (FDC ID 172686). Mayonnaise, pickled vegetables, soy sauce, and chilli paste contributions are incorporated proportionally. Per-100 g values represent the composite assembled sandwich. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — white bread and chicken sandwich analogues
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson FS et al., Diabetes Care 2021
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Vietnamese-style shredded chicken bánh mì sandwich. GI estimated at 55 based on the dominant carbohydrate source being a French-style baguette (white wheat bread GI approximately 57–75, University of Sydney GI Database) moderated by the protein and fat content of shredded chicken and mayonnaise, which slow gastric emptying and reduce the effective glycemic response. A composite GI of 55 (medium) is defensible per the mixed-meal GI estimation methodology described in Atkinson et al. (2021). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (55 × 49.3 g carbohydrate per 220 g serving) / 100 = 27.1.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from soy sauce, mayonnaise, and pickled vegetables
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit consumption of this sandwich due to its estimated sodium content of approximately 520 mg per 100 g (approximately 1,144 mg per serving), which represents nearly 50% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2,300 mg; requesting reduced soy sauce and pickled vegetables can meaningfully lower sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index driven by refined white baguette bread, partially offset by protein and fat content
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that this sandwich has a medium GI of approximately 55 and a glycemic load of approximately 27 per serving, which is clinically significant; portion control, choosing a smaller serving, and pairing with additional non-starchy vegetables can help moderate postprandial blood glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Moderate saturated fat from mayonnaise and moderate sodium from soy sauce and pickled condiments
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals managing cardiovascular disease risk, this sandwich provides moderate saturated fat (approximately 1.6 g per 100 g) and high sodium; opting for reduced-fat mayonnaise, limiting soy sauce, and increasing the proportion of fresh vegetables within the sandwich can improve the cardiovascular risk profile.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Shredded Chicken Bánh Mì Sandwich (Bánh Mì Gà Xé) is a Vietnamese-influenced sandwich served in Singapore, most commonly associated with Vietnamese-Singaporean eateries. It consists of a crispy French-style baguette filled with tender hand-shredded poached or braised chicken breast, homemade or commercial mayonnaise, soy sauce, quick-pickled daikon radish and carrot, fresh cucumber slices, fragrant cilantro (coriander leaves), and chilli paste or fresh sliced chilli. The baguette provides a light, airy crumb with a crisp crust, while the shredded chicken offers a moist, savoury filling. The pickled vegetables contribute acidity and crunch that balance the richness of the mayonnaise. Nutritionally, the sandwich is a moderate-energy food providing approximately 218 kcal per 100 g, with a meaningful protein contribution of 13.5 g per 100 g from the chicken. The sodium content is elevated due to soy sauce and pickled condiments, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The medium glycemic index of approximately 55, driven by the refined white baguette, is clinically relevant for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes. The dish is generally considered halal and dairy-free but contains gluten and eggs (from mayonnaise). It is a popular lunch and light dinner option in Singapore's Vietnamese restaurant scene.
common_user_queries: What is bánh mì gà xé?
Is shredded chicken banh mi healthy?
How many calories in a chicken banh mi sandwich?
Is banh mi high in sodium?
What is in a Vietnamese chicken sandwich?

confidence_reason: No exact entry for a Vietnamese-style shredded chicken bánh mì sandwich was found in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy chicken sandwich data and component-level USDA entries for cooked shredded chicken breast and baguette bread. GI is an analogous estimate based on the dominant carbohydrate source (white baguette) moderated by protein and fat, using University of Sydney GI Database reference values. Medium confidence is appropriate given the composite estimation approach.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is set to -1 as it is not reliably reported in the USDA SR Legacy chicken sandwich source entry and is not expected to be a significant contributor in this composite dish. All other -1 values in per_100g (monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g) have been replaced with estimated numeric values derived from USDA component data and are not sentinel values. The food is sold at Co Chung Restaurant - Lorong Telok on field survey data at SGD 16.48. The Vietnamese name Bánh Mì Gà Xé is preserved from the input record. The dish reflects the Vietnamese culinary influence present in Singapore's food scene. Halal status is assumed based on typical preparation at Vietnamese-Singaporean restaurants but should be verified at the specific restaurant level.

q1_food_identity.notes: Canonical English food name 'Shredded Chicken Bánh Mì Sandwich' is precise and non-promotional. Vietnamese local name Bánh Mì Gà Xé is present. All mandatory Singaporean-cuisine language names are present: English (food_name), Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi.

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: All four core macros (energy_kcal 218.0, protein_g 13.5, fat_g 8.2, carbohydrate_g 22.4) are present as numeric values. Nutrient source is USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy adapted for composite bánh mì profile. Source match type is 'adapted' with full explanation.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g (1.4), sodium_mg (520.0), saturated_fat_g (1.6), and sugar_g (2.8) are all present as numeric values and are plausible for a chicken sandwich with soy sauce and pickled vegetables.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: glycemic_index is 55 (numeric, greater than zero), gi_category is 'medium', glycemic_load_per_serving is 27.1, gi_source references University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021 peer-reviewed journal. Analogous estimate basis is fully documented.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn FOODON_00002473 (chicken sandwich) used as closest_parent with gap note. FoodEx2 A0FHR (cooked chicken meat) used as closest_parent with gap note. SNOMED CT is null with match_type 'none' and gap note explaining absence. No codes are invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description is 1,247 characters, well above the 200-character minimum. It covers what the food is, preparation method, taste and texture, cultural context, clinical relevance, and key nutrients.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions are present: Hypertension (high concern, sodium), Type 2 Diabetes (moderate concern, medium GI), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate concern, saturated fat and sodium). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and a specific clinical guidance sentence. human_condition_name for type2_diabetes is 'Type 2 Diabetes'.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: aliases list contains 15 entries covering English variants, Vietnamese script and romanized forms, Chinese simplified and traditional, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi. aliases_by_language is fully populated for all relevant languages. Every string in aliases_by_language has been verified to appear verbatim in the flat aliases list. Consistency check passed.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — All six hard gates pass. Score 8/8. Food identity is precise with full Singaporean-cuisine multilingual coverage (English, Chinese simplified and traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) plus Vietnamese. Nutrients are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy with full provenance documentation. GI of 55 (medium) is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database reference values with full methodology documented. Ontology uses closest_parent codes with honest gap notes and no invented codes. LLM description exceeds 200 characters with clinical and cultural detail. All three health conditions are present with specific clinical guidance. Aliases are comprehensive and fully consistent between aliases_by_language and flat aliases list.
